programmes.show_description: 1985 Christmas special. Alf schemes so they can attend two Christmas dinners. Rita is forced to stay.

20th-century technology probed alleged hauntings, yet ghosts remain unproven. This programme reviews sightings and tests rational explanations. Narrated by Robert Powell.
With the busy season coming to a close, the team may still be in for a bumpy ride as they prepare for the railway's biggest steam event ever and Piglet's job comes under scrutiny. S3 E2
The Yakovlev Yak-1 was a Soviet fighter aircraft of World War II - it was a single-seat monoplane with a composite structure and wooden wings and was a fast and competitive fighter aircraft. S3 E4
The story about the family of the man who embodies the darkest pages in history, this film puts an accent on his legacy as it stands today; who are the heirs of Hitler, and where do they live today?
Theories surrounding the death of Hermann Goering, who managed to cheat the hangman, and his relationship with chief military psychiatrist Lt Colonel Douglas M Kelley. Ep5: Hermann Goering
The final phase of the Battle of Britain takes place - one Londoner in every six is made homeless, but Hitler fails to gain air supremacy and his invasion is cancelled. Narrated by Michael Aspel. E2
A collection of recorded interviews where WW1 veterans bring to life the reality of the Battle of the Somme, the catastrophic campaign that began in the summer of 1916. S1 E2: The Battle of The Somme

programmes.show_name: Living in the Shadow of WWII
programmes.show_description: Combatant countries go to great lengths to maintain their people's morale, offering diversion and entertainment while reassuring citizens that their cause is just and victory certain. Ep3: Happy Days
The landing at Anzac Cove in April 1915 was part of the amphibious invasion of the Gallipoli Peninsula by the forces of the British Empire and began the land phase of the First World War.
...Were You? Mount Everest is the greatest climbing challenge in the world, thought unconquerable by many until Sir Edmund Hillary, on May 29th, 1953, changed that perception and reached the summit.

We examine the frustrations in 1916 & 1917 of a bogged down war and its repercussions with the fighting forces and leadership of both sides involved in the conflict and some of the lessons learned. E8
A dramatic exploration of how Hitler consistently cheated death. S1 E1: How Hitler's Bodyguard Worked.
Moving through our countdown of America's greatest unsolved mysteries and crimes, we uncover a secret society, and two baffling tales of disappearance - an American hero and a whole culture. E8
Jack looks after a socialist lecturer who is speaking at the Co-Op Hall, and Billy takes Bill to a New Year's party at Jessie's against his will. S1 E11: Happy New Year, Some Say
Arthur acquires a hot air balloon in a bid to break into the aerial advertising game, but his latest venture is hijacked. S9 E2: No Way to Treat a Daley
With Jim gravely ill in Bodmin jail, Ross and Dwight launch a desperate mission to save him; meanwhile, Demelza is excited to attend a grand ball held by George. Starring Aidan Turner. S1 E6
Drama series about a detective who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. When Sam responds to a stabbing, he finds it's at the converted mill where he lives in 2006. Starring John Simm. S1 E3
Alf returns home from the funeral of Else, his long-suffering wife. Having lost her disability allowance and half the pension, life ahead is not too rosy. S2 E1)
With Elvis Costello and David Essex. The legendary BAFTA-winning music and comedy show from Thames TV (1979). All in the best possible taste!

While in South America, Simon discovers a pair of con artists who stole a friend's savings and teaches them a valuable lesson while getting involved with local revolutionaries. S3 E19: The Golden Frog

Exploring claims of miraculous religious statues. Moving, weeping, healing, is this faith, illusion, or a miracle? Narrated by Robert Powell.
The heat is on during a hot bank holiday as an annual steam gala weekend approaches and keeping the 140-tonne engines moving safely proves a challenge. S3 E3
The F-4 Phantom II is an American tandem two-seat, twin-engine, long-range supersonic jet interceptor and fighter-bomber originally developed by McDonnell Aircraft for the United States Navy. S3 E5
Revealing life for guests and staff in a typical three-star British holiday hotel. S1 E1
The theories, conspiracies and facts relating to Rudolf Hess's dramatic flight to Scotland in 1941 and his subsequent death in Spandau prison. Ep6: Rudolf Hess
We look back to the bombing of London and other cities by the Luftwaffe in 1940 and 1941 by examining life between air raids and the effects they had on British society. Narrated by Michael Aspel. E3
The aftermath of every battle was human carnage. We hear the heartbreaking and demanding stories of the doctors and medical orderlies who tended to the wounded and dying. S1 E3: Saving The Wounded
In 1954, the USA exploded a hydrogen bomb in the South Pacific - a few years later, the USSR detonated a device even larger. We examine why these superpowers raced to build the world's biggest bomb.
The Battle of the Somme began in July 1916; more than three million men fought in the battle and one million men were wounded or killed, making it one of the deadliest battles in human history.
Codenamed Operation Overlord, the Battle of Normandy in 1944 - the invasion of Nazi-occupied Western Europe by the Allies - is one of the best-known battles of World War II and also known as D-Day.
We explore how the change of attitude on the political front in the USA and incidents involving Americans with combatants in the on-going fight led the United States to join this global conflict. E9
As Adolf Hitler clawed his way closer to power, his extremist policies brought him a host of enemies. S1 E2: Early Attempts on Hitler's Life
We reach the top 10 in our countdown of America's greatest unsolved mysteries including the assassination of Martin Luther King, a witch hunt in Salem, and the missing minutes on Nixon's tapes. E9
Jack helps Tom take on two ruffians who threaten him over money, and Jessie tries to persuade a widow to let her son take up a scholarship. S1 E12: Heads You Win, Tails I Lose
Arthur starts a courier service, the Daley Post, and it isn't long before its competitors start throwing the wrong sort of business at him. S9 E3: Uneasy Rider
Francis wrongly blames Ross for helping Verity elope with Andrew Blamey, and Mark suspects Dwight and Keren of having an affair and starts a chain of events. Starring Aidan Turner. S1 E7
Sam uncovers corruption in CID when he takes on a local crime boss. When the crime boss takes steps to stop Sam's investigation, will Gene do what is right? Starring John Simm. S1 E4
